# Operating Laws

| Code | Law | Rule |
| --- | --- | --- |
| L1 | Compression before styling | Rewrite speech into meaning before applying any visual treatment. |
| L2 | Signal structure, not decoration | Highlight the relation that matters: Not X → Y, Problem → Cause → Fix, Stop X → Start Y. |
| L3 | Protect gaze-critical regions | Do not cover eyes, mouth, hands, product, action, or platform UI when those regions carry information. |
| L4 | Adapt density to visual load | Use more words on simple talking-head shots and fewer words or labels on visually busy shots. |
| L5 | Use bionic anchors as a testable component | Partial-word emphasis is not assumed to work; it must beat semantic compression alone. |
| L6 | Preserve accessibility | Full captions remain available; semantic overlays are an enhancement layer. |
| L7 | Proof is comparative | A method is accepted only when it beats its named comparator on its predicted metric. |
| L8 | Measure meaning efficiency | Optimize meaning retained per second and, where possible, meaning retained per fixation. |
